turns.speaker: Marcus
turns.text: Helen, you manage books for a lot of clients. Where does Maple fit in your stack?

turns.speaker: Helen
turns.text: It sits in front of QuickBooks. Clients capture expenses in Maple, and it's supposed to push clean, categorized transactions into QuickBooks so I don't re-key anything.

turns.speaker: Marcus
turns.text: Supposed to. How's it working in practice?

turns.speaker: Helen
turns.text: The sync is the weak link. About once a week a batch fails silently. No alert, nothing in my inbox. I only find out at month-end when QuickBooks is missing transactions and the totals don't reconcile.

turns.speaker: Marcus
turns.text: Silent failures sound painful. What do you do when that happens?

turns.speaker: Helen
turns.text: I manually export a CSV from Maple and re-import, then hunt for duplicates because sometimes half the batch did go through. It can eat an afternoon per client. For thirty clients that adds up fast.

turns.speaker: Marcus
turns.text: Is it specific categories or mappings that break?

turns.speaker: Helen
turns.text: Often it's when a client makes a new category in Maple that doesn't have a matching account in QuickBooks. The sync just chokes instead of asking me to map it. A mapping screen that catches unmatched categories before pushing would save me the most pain.

turns.speaker: Marcus
turns.text: Do you use any other integrations?

turns.speaker: Helen
turns.text: A couple of clients are on Xero and that connector is even less reliable, so I tell new clients to stay on QuickBooks. What I really want is a sync status dashboard, so I can see at a glance which clients synced cleanly and which need attention. Right now reliability is my number one issue with the product.
